description:
You are an observability engineer who has to update an existing dashboard
    definition stored as JSON.  
    The file that is currently deployed is

        /home/user/dashboards/current/k8s_nodes.json

    A colleague has given you an improved version located at

        /home/user/dashboards/proposed/k8s_nodes.json

    1. Create a unified text-diff (a normal GNU “diff -u” patch) between the
       *current* and the *proposed* versions and store that patch as

        /home/user/dashboards/patches/k8s_nodes.patch

       • The patch **must** be in standard unified format (starts with
         “--- …​”, “+++ …​”, uses “@@” hunk headers, etc.).  
       • The patch file has to contain the text “Disk usage” somewhere inside
         it (this is part of the new panel that is being added).

    2. Apply the patch so that
       /home/user/dashboards/current/k8s_nodes.json
       is modified to match the proposed file exactly.

    3. Generate a verification log at

        /home/user/dashboards/logs/patch_apply.log

       The log must be **exactly two lines long** (no trailing blank lines):

           Line 1:  PATCH_APPLIED_SUCCESSFULLY  
           Line 2:  UPDATED_FILE_LINES:21

       • Note: “21” is the exact number of lines in the final
         k8s_nodes.json after the patch.  
       • Use the literal strings shown above (all caps, no extra
         whitespace).

    The automated test will check that  
    a) the patch file exists and contains the string “Disk usage”,  
    b) the current/k8s_nodes.json file is byte-for-byte identical to the
       proposed one, and  
    c) /​home/​user/​dashboards/​logs/​patch_apply.log contains the two
       required lines *and nothing else*.

    Hint: this entire task can be completed with two or three commands
    (generate a diff, apply the patch, then write the log).
truth:
Initial file: /home/user/dashboards/current/k8s_nodes.json
    ----------------------------------------------------------
    {
      "title": "Kubernetes Nodes",
      "panels": [
        {
          "type": "graph",
          "name": "CPU usage",
          "query": "sum(rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total[5m])) by (node)"
        },
        {
          "type": "graph",
          "name": "Memory usage",
          "query": "sum(container_memory_usage_bytes) by (node)"
        }
      ],
      "refresh": "5s"
    }

    Proposed file: /home/user/dashboards/proposed/k8s_nodes.json
    ------------------------------------------------------------
    {
      "title": "Kubernetes Nodes",
      "panels": [
        {
          "type": "graph",
          "name": "CPU usage",
          "query": "sum(rate(container_cpu_usage_seconds_total[5m])) by (node)"
        },
        {
          "type": "graph",
          "name": "Memory usage",
          "query": "sum(container_memory_usage_bytes) by (node)"
        },
        {
          "type": "graph",
          "name": "Disk usage",
          "query": "sum(container_fs_usage_bytes) by (node)"
        }
      ],
      "refresh": "10s"
    }

    The proposed JSON is exactly 21 lines long (LF line endings).

    After successful completion:
    --------------------------------
    1. /home/user/dashboards/patches/k8s_nodes.patch exists and is a valid
       unified diff converting the initial file to the proposed one.  
       It contains the string “Disk usage”.

    2. /home/user/dashboards/current/k8s_nodes.json is byte-identical to
       /home/user/dashboards/proposed/k8s_nodes.json (21 lines, refresh set
       to “10s”, includes the new “Disk usage” panel).

    3. /home/user/dashboards/logs/patch_apply.log contents
       (two lines, no trailing newline after the second line):

           PATCH_APPLIED_SUCCESSFULLY
           UPDATED_FILE_LINES:21
name: task_000000_f6624c46
